title: alchemilla mollis
intro: Alchemilla mollis, also known as lady's mantle or common lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant in the rose family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds, eczema, and other skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla mollis include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair, which can lead to various health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced risk of infection. Alchemilla mollis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
